ghost_patient_status: Re-evaluation triggered by new oncology progress note in EHR
expected_reasoning: Patient started DRd in February (now exposed to lenalidomide) but is currently RESPONDING — not refractory. Cannot yet establish lenalidomide-refractoriness. NEEDS_REVIEW until either response is durable (eligibility remains blocked) or progression occurs (becomes len-refractory and eligible).

documents.author: R. Singh, MD
documents.content:
73M IgG kappa MM, dx 5/2022. Now 3rd line of therapy.

UPDATED TX HX:
- L1 (6/2022 - 4/2023): VCD x6. PD.
- L2 (5/2023 - 1/2026): KCd. PD.
- L3 (2/2026 - present): DRd (daratumumab + lenalidomide + dex). Started 2/22/2026 after extensive discussion of risk/benefit given renal status. Lenalidomide dose-reduced to 10mg given CKD (CrCl ~30). Patient has now received 2.5 cycles.

Response to date: M-spike trending DOWN (was 2.4 g/dL pre-tx, now 1.6 g/dL at C2). sFLC ratio improving. No new bone disease on recent imaging. EARLY PARTIAL RESPONSE.

LENALIDOMIDE STATUS: now EXPOSED (since 2/22/26), but currently RESPONDING. Not refractory. Has not progressed on lenalidomide.

Tolerance: Pt tolerating reduced-dose len with G-CSF support. ANC was nadir 0.8 after C1, recovered with filgrastim. No infections. Cr stable at 1.7.

ECOG 1. Independent ADLs. Wife well.

Labs 5/6/26: Hgb 10.6, ANC 1.9 (post-G-CSF), plt 95. Cr 1.7 CrCl ~30. M-spike 1.6, sFLC kappa 65.

A/P: 73M r/r MM on L3 DRd, early PR, tolerating. Continue DRd. Re-image at C4. Re-assess CAR-T candidacy IF/WHEN progression on lenalidomide-containing regimen occurs.
